questions.question: Who is authorized to create and edit groups in a workspace?

options.text: All workspace members
options.explanation: While members can be added to groups, only workspace owners and membership admins have the permission to create and edit them.

options.text: Workspace owners and membership admins
options.explanation: According to the article, workspace owners and membership admins are the only roles allowed to create and edit groups.

options.text: Anyone with a link to the workspace
options.explanation: Guests and people with links generally do not have administrative privileges to create groups.

options.text: Only the workspace owner
options.explanation: Membership admins also have the authority to create and edit groups.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is true regarding group members?

options.text: Groups can contain an unlimited number of guests
options.explanation: Groups cannot contain workspace guests; only workspace members can be assigned to groups.

options.text: Workspace members can only be assigned to one group
options.explanation: The article does not state a limit on the number of groups a single member can be part of.

options.text: Groups can be created from the People section without needing an admin
options.explanation: You must have workspace owner or membership admin privileges to create groups.

options.text: You can search for a specific person to see which group they belong to
options.explanation: The article notes that you can search someone's name or email to see which group they are in.

questions.question: When sharing a page with a group, which permission level allows the group to share the page with others?

options.text: Can comment
options.explanation: Can comment allows read and comment access, but not editing or sharing.

options.text: Can view
options.explanation: Can view allows only reading the content; the user cannot share it.

options.text: Full access
options.explanation: Full access grants the group the ability to edit the page and share it.

options.text: Can edit
options.explanation: Can edit allows editing the content but explicitly prevents sharing (available on paid plans).

questions.question: How do you share a teamspace with groups rather than individual members?

options.text: Go to Settings → People → Groups and select the teamspace
options.explanation: Groups are managed within the People or Members section, but teamspace settings are accessed via a different menu.

options.text: Hover over the teamspace name and click ••• → Teamspace settings
options.explanation: To share a teamspace with groups, you must hover over the teamspace in the sidebar and access Teamspace settings.

options.text: Click the Share button on the first page inside the teamspace
options.explanation: Sharing a page inside a teamspace shares it with individuals, not the entire teamspace structure.

options.text: Invite the groups directly from the workspace settings menu
options.explanation: Workspace settings are for general workspace configuration, not specific teamspace sharing.

questions.question: What is a specific limitation of the 'Can edit' permission level?

options.text: They cannot edit the page content at all
options.explanation: The 'Can edit' permission specifically allows users to edit the page's content.

options.text: They can edit the page content, but they cannot share the page
options.explanation: The article states that 'Can edit' allows editing content but prevents sharing the page (available on paid plans).

options.text: They cannot mention the group in other pages
options.explanation: Mentioning capabilities are separate from page-level sharing permissions.

options.text: They cannot see the page at all
options.explanation: If they have access to edit, they can see the page.

questions.question: How can you set an icon for a newly created group?

options.text: Click the settings icon next to the group name
options.explanation: The settings icon is usually for renaming or deleting, not setting an icon.

options.text: Click the emoji icon that appears on hover
options.explanation: The article instructs to click the emoji icon that appears on hover to set an icon.

options.text: The icon is automatically assigned based on the group name
options.explanation: The icon must be manually selected by the user.

options.text: You cannot set an icon for groups
options.explanation: The article explicitly mentions setting an icon via the emoji icon.

questions.question: When using groups to separate teams without multiple workspaces, what is the first step in the recommended workaround?

options.text: Create a top-level page for the engineering team
options.explanation: Creating the page comes after disabling workspace access.

options.text: Disable Workspace access
options.explanation: The workaround begins by disabling Workspace access so pages don't show for unauthorized groups.

options.text: Grant Full access to the engineering group
options.explanation: Granting access is done after the top-level page is created.

options.text: Rename the existing workspace
options.explanation: Renaming the workspace is not part of the workaround.

questions.question: How do you mention a user group in a database person property?

options.text: Type # followed by the group name
options.explanation: The hashtag is used for hashtags, not group mentions.

options.text: Type @ followed by the group name
options.explanation: Groups are mentioned by typing @ followed by the group name.

options.text: Type $ followed by the group name
options.explanation: The dollar sign is not used for group mentions.

options.text: Type > followed by the group name
options.explanation: The greater than symbol is not used for group mentions.

questions.question: If you give a group 'Full access' to a top-level page, what is the effect on nested sub-pages?

options.text: They inherit the 'Can view' permission automatically
options.explanation: Permissions cascade, but they inherit the parent's permission, not a restrictive one.

options.text: They are not affected by the parent page's permissions
options.explanation: Permissions on any page also apply to all of their sub-pages.

options.text: You must manually set permissions for every sub-page
options.explanation: The article states that permissions on the parent page apply to sub-pages.

options.text: They inherit the 'Full access' permission
options.explanation: The article notes that permissions on any page also apply to all of their sub-pages.

questions.question: In the workaround for using groups instead of multiple workspaces, what specific action allows you to prevent unauthorized groups from seeing the workspace?

options.text: Delete the guests from the workspace
options.explanation: Deleting guests does not prevent groups from seeing workspace content.

options.text: Disable Workspace access
options.explanation: Disabling Workspace access ensures pages won't show for people and groups who aren't specifically granted access.

options.text: Rename the workspace to 'Private'
options.explanation: Renaming the workspace does not restrict access to specific groups.

options.text: Remove all members from the workspace
options.explanation: This would break the workspace functionality.
